summary: Most children stop thumb sucking around 4 years but can continue longer; there’s significant individual variation noted among kids regarding when they cease this behavior completely. The typical range for stopping ranges from approximately two to four years, though some may persist beyond these limits due to varying factors influencing each child individually. To summarize concisely while capturing essential information related directly back towards addressing original query posed earlier concerning duration over which thumb sucking tends predominantly occur within pediatric population context provided here initially via given options presented previously including specific reference made toward timing associated closely linked specifically tied into proposed answer choice labeled as "first 4 years".
